courses.title: AI se Baat Kaise Karte Hai
courses.subtitle: Prompt Engineering for Everyone
courses.summary: Talk to AI like a pro. Frameworks for prompts that get real work done.
outcomes: Master 7 prompt frameworks used in real jobs
Build a personal prompt library you reuse daily
Get the same task done 5x faster with AI
modules: Why prompts matter (think like a manager, not a typist)
The 7 frameworks: RACE, CRISP, RTF, COSTAR, CARE, BAB, PAS
Long-context prompts: feed PDFs, sheets, code, websites
Workflows: research, writing, summarising, comparing, deciding
Your personal prompt library + Notion template

courses.title: AI for Social Media Marketing
courses.subtitle: Content, scheduling, hooks & analytics with AI
courses.summary: Automate captions, hooks, scheduling and reels research with AI agents.
outcomes: Run 5 brand pages without burning out
Build a content engine: hook → caption → schedule → analytics
Save 15+ hours/week with reusable AI workflows
modules: The new SMM operator role (AI does the typing, you decide)
Hook mining + caption frameworks with Claude / GPT
Analytics: weekly report agent that writes itself
Capstone: full brand calendar in 3 hours

courses.title: AI for Branding

Voice + tone with AI: prompts that don’t sound robotic
Brand kit: templates, social, packaging mockups

Build a job-ready portfolio piece

courses.title: AI in Depth (Technical)

payload.assistant_message: Wow, 5 brands — that's solid volume! Are you doing everything manually (content creation, captions, scheduling) or already using some tools? Also, curious — what's your biggest headache right now: ideation, consistency, or analytics?

payload.assistant_message: Got it — so you're doing captions, scheduling, and ad analytics daily. Quick question: Are you currently using any AI tools for these tasks, or is it mostly manual work right now?

risk.headline: Your repetitive tasks are prime targets for AI automation within 12-18 months
reasons: Caption writing is rapidly being automated by GPT-4 and specialized marketing LLMs
Scheduling and performance reviews are already handled end-to-end by AI-powered platforms
Manual execution of these tasks offers little strategic differentiation
safe_zones: Strategic thinking — understanding brand voice and audience psychology
Creative direction and high-level campaign concepts
Relationship building with influencers and community management

recommended_courses.why: Learn to automate captions, scheduling and analytics so you focus on strategy, not execution
Master prompts that generate on-brand captions faster than manual writing

payload.assistant_message: That's a red flag — competitors using AI are already 5-10x faster on the same tasks. One more thing: Do you also handle strategy (like planning campaigns, choosing platforms, setting KPIs), or is your role mostly execution of what others decide?

risk.headline: Without AI adoption, you're competing at a severe speed and cost disadvantage
reasons: Manual caption writing takes 15-30 min per post; AI does it in 30 seconds with equal or better quality
Employers are already replacing non-AI social media managers with AI-powered alternatives or smaller teams
Your manual workflow makes you 10x more expensive per output than AI-equipped peers
safe_zones: Deep brand understanding and voice consistency across campaigns
Audience insight and emotional intelligence in messaging
Crisis management and real-time community engagement

recommended_courses.why: Immediately close the speed gap — automate captions, scheduling and analytics to stay competitive
Learn the foundational skill of talking to AI so you control it instead of being replaced by it
